description_long: This object is a classic percussion cap revolver, featuring a long, octagonal barrel and a six-chambered rotating cylinder. The barrel is metallic with a dark, slightly worn finish, and the frame and cylinder are robust, likely made of blued steel. The grip is crafted from dark wood, smoothly contoured for ergonomic handling, and contrasts with the metallic components. The trigger guard is rounded and metallic, enclosing a simple trigger mechanism. The hammer is exposed and prominently arched, designed for manual cocking. The overall design is reminiscent of 19th-century Colt or Remington revolvers, with intricate details and a solid, utilitarian build.
description: A classic percussion cap revolver with a long, octagonal barrel, dark blued steel frame, and a smooth wooden grip. The design is robust and detailed, evoking 19th-century craftsmanship.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular barrel opening, the front sight post, and the symmetrical alignment of the barrel and cylinder.
description_view_1: Right side view displaying the full length of the octagonal barrel, the six-chambered cylinder, the wooden grip, and the exposed hammer.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the back of the grip, the hammer, and the alignment of the barrel and cylinder from behind.
description_view_3: Left side view showing the same features as the right, with clear visibility of the trigger guard, cylinder, and wooden grip.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, long octagonal barrel with no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized metallic trigger guard with smooth edges and no finger grooves
